---
title: "Game Time"
subtitle: "Match Metadata to Darwin Core"
author: "Emilie Boulanger, Elizabeth Lawrence"
date: 2026-07-22
---

## Match Metadata to Darwin Core

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
📋 **Setup**

- Groups of 4–5
- Box of field + methodology fragments
- 4 posters per DwC table: Event · Occurrence · DNA · eMoF
    - Each table has a pile of DwC column headers. Not all are relevant! Find the one(s) that are
:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
🎯 **Task**

- Read a fragment out loud as a group
- Decide: which **table**? which **column** (i.e. term)?
- Place it in the right spot
- Fill in the information with the right vocab

⚠️ **Watch For**

- Some fragments may split across 2+ columns
- Some fragments may not belong in any table
:::
::::

::: {.notes}
Facilitator notes: remind participants that DwC mapping is often a judgment call — several fragments (e.g., filter method, preservation buffer) are genuinely ambiguous between samplingProtocol and the DNA extension's sop field, and that's fine. The goal isn't a single "correct" answer for every card, it's practicing the reasoning process: read the raw text, identify what kind of information it is (event-level, occurrence-level, molecular method, or measurement), and find its home. After 20 minutes, walk the room and reveal the answer key live, pausing longest on posters where groups disagreed.
:::

## eDNA data for national monitoring

A national monitoring programme wants all eDNA data publicly available within one year.

:::: {.columns}
::: {.column width="50%"}
**Who are you?**

Each of you will get a role, representing one of these stakeholder categories: 

    🌳 Field ecologist
    🧬 Molecular laboratory scientist / Bioinformatician
    💻 Data manager
    🏛️ Repository curator
    📜 Policymaker
    🌐 Biodiversity database representative
    🗺️ End user

:::

::: {.column width="50%"}
👥 **What do you need?**

Within your stakeholder role, you should discuss and explain:

    - **What you need?**
    - **What concerns you?**
    - **What data you can provide?**
    - **What standards you require?**


:::
::::
